methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Orchid, a Series A biotech & health company founded by Noor Siddiqui, has raised $16.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in the sector, including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Given data constraints, only the stage-progression method proved applicable. Using total capital raised ($16.5M) as a proxy for post-money valuation at Series A entry, a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to Series B produces an implied valuation of $33M. The peer-median approach could not be deployed due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting the quality threshold. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (6.9x median across the three public tickers) was inapplicable without disclosed revenue for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $28M to $38M, with a central estimate of $33M. This range reflects ±15% around the stage-progression midpoint. The primary uncertainty stems from reliance on a single methodological pillar and the absence of direct revenue or earnings data; a fuller view would require either disclosure of Orchid's financials or identification of additional private-company comparables at tighter valuation proximity. Serial-stage capital data and final-round pricing terms, if available, would strengthen conviction in this range.
